homepage.heroSubline: Precision welding for homes and businesses across Metro Detroit. Built to last through Michigan winters and beyond.
aboutText: Legacy Welding started in 2012 when founder Mike Kowalski saw Detroit's infrastructure needed welders who understood local conditions. What began as one truck is now a 20-person crew serving Wayne, Oakland, and Washtenaw counties.
We weld like Detroiters build - with grit, precision, and no shortcuts. Every joint is inspected, every material matched to Michigan's freeze-thaw cycles and corrosive winters.
From Corktown lofts to Rochester Hills subdivisions, we're invested in this community. Our team lives here, our kids go to school here, and we stand behind every weld.

valueProps.title: Michigan-Proven Methods
valueProps.description: Our techniques account for lake-effect snow loads, road salt corrosion, and temperature swings from -10°F to 90°F.

valueProps.title: Right Materials
valueProps.description: We specify alloys and coatings that withstand Detroit's harsh winters without premature failure.

valueProps.title: No Callback Guarantee
valueProps.description: If our weld fails under normal conditions within 5 years, we'll repair it at no cost.

valueProps.title: Same-Day Emergency
valueProps.description: When a critical structural weld fails in January, we'll be there within 24 hours.

homepage.ctaHeadline: Need a Welder Who Gets Detroit?
homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-WEL-RQ12 for immediate service or a free project estimate.

services.name: Structural Steel Repair
services.shortDescription: Restore compromised steel beams, columns, and connections in Detroit-area buildings.
services.description:
Michigan winters take a brutal toll on structural steel. Between road salt corrosion and freeze-thaw cycles, we see failing connections in everything from Corktown warehouses to Troy office buildings.

Our process starts with abrasive blasting to remove all rust, followed by ultrasonic thickness testing. We then reinforce with high-strength steel plates welded using low-hydrogen electrodes to prevent cold cracking. Final coatings include zinc-rich primers and industrial urethanes matched to your building's existing systems.

Common projects include: repairing cracked beam-to-column connections in Midtown lofts, reinforcing sagging garage headers in Royal Oak bungalows, and replacing corroded steel lintels above brick facades.
process: Step 1: Inspect with magnetic particle testing to identify hidden cracks
Step 2: Shore up loads with temporary supports if needed
Step 3: Weld reinforcement plates using preheat when temps are below 40°F
Step 4: Apply corrosion-resistant finish matching existing surfaces
benefits: Prevents catastrophic structural failures during heavy snow loads
Extends service life 15+ years with proper corrosion protection
Meets Michigan building code requirements for seismic retrofits
Minimizes business downtime with phased repair scheduling

faq.question: How do you prevent weld failure in freezing temps?
faq.answer: We preheat base metals to 150°F minimum and use low-hydrogen electrodes stored in heated quivers. Post-weld, we wrap joints in insulating blankets for slow cooling.

faq.question: Can you match historic steel profiles?
faq.answer: Yes - our metal shop rolls custom steel shapes to replicate original early 20th century structural elements in Detroit's historic districts.

services.name: Residential Metal Fabrication
services.shortDescription: Custom wrought iron, steel, and aluminum features for Metro Detroit homes.
services.description:
From Palmer Woods estates to Ann Arbor student housing, we fabricate metal features that stand up to Michigan's climate.

Our shop crafts everything from frost-resistant balcony railings to snow-load-rated pergolas using 11-gauge steel or 6061-T6 aluminum. For historic districts like Indian Village, we replicate original wrought iron patterns while upgrading to modern corrosion-resistant finishes.

Recent projects include: Lake-effect-snow-rated steel carports in Rochester Hills, salt-resistant aluminum stair systems for lakefront homes, and freeze-thaw-stable steel planter boxes for Midtown condos.

All residential work includes hidden drainage channels to prevent ice dam formation and concealed fasteners that won't stain brick or stone.
process: Step 1: Site measurements with 3D scanning for precision fits
Step 2: CAD design showing snow load calculations
Step 3: Fabrication using press brakes and CNC plasma cutting
Step 4: Installation with expansion joints for seasonal movement
benefits: Materials selected for minimal winter thermal bridging
Hidden fasteners prevent brick spalling damage
Drainage channels eliminate ice buildup
Powder coating lasts 3x longer than standard paint

faq.question: Do your railings meet Michigan building codes?
faq.answer: All guardrails are built to withstand 200 lb concentrated loads per Detroit residential code, with baluster spacing that prevents child entrapment.

faq.question: How long does powder coating last?
faq.answer: Our 3-stage coating system typically lasts 15+ years even with road salt exposure, compared to 5 years for standard paint.

services.name: Commercial Welding
services.shortDescription: Code-compliant welding for Detroit-area businesses and institutions.
services.description:
When your business needs welding that inspectors approve without hesitation, our certified welders deliver.

We specialize in Michigan-specific commercial solutions: salt-resistant stainless steel handrails for DIA visitors, snow-load-rated steel canopies for Belle Isle pavilions, and vibration-resistant welds for manufacturing plants along I-94.

Our team holds AWS D1.1 and D1.5 certifications for structural and bridge welding. We maintain strict procedures for weld sequencing on sensitive projects like hospital renovations or data center upgrades.

Every commercial project includes: certified welder testing reports, as-built drawings for facilities teams, and coordination with your fire suppression system during hot work.
process: Step 1: Review Michigan building code requirements for your occupancy type
Step 2: Perform mock-up welds for client/inspector approval
Step 3: Implement phased welding to control distortion
Step 4: Provide certified welder continuity records
benefits: Minimizes business interruption with night/weekend work
Welder certifications available for client records
Seismic detailing for critical facilities
Vibration control during sensitive operations

faq.question: Can you weld during winter months?
faq.answer: Yes - we erect temporary enclosures with heaters to maintain proper welding temperatures even in January.

faq.question: Do you carry insurance for sensitive sites?
faq.answer: Our $5M umbrella policy meets requirements for schools, hospitals, and government facilities.

services.name: Historic Restoration Welding
services.shortDescription: Preservation-grade metalwork for Detroit's architectural treasures.
services.description:
Corktown's iron facades and Midtown's steel windows demand welders who respect historic fabric while making lasting repairs.

We're one of few Detroit shops approved for historic district work, using period-appropriate techniques like forge welding on pre-1920s ironwork. Our team consults preservation architects to match original profiles while upgrading hidden structural elements.

Recent projects include: repairing 1890s cast iron columns using bronze welding rods, replicating missing wrought iron balcony elements with hand-forged steel, and stabilizing Art Deco steel windows with concealed stainless steel reinforcement.

All work complies with National Park Service standards for historic preservation and qualifies for Michigan historic tax credits.
process: Step 1: Historic materials analysis (often including metallurgy testing)
Step 2: Documentation with photogrammetry before disassembly
Step 3: In-situ repairs when possible to preserve patina
Step 4: Reassembly using reversible mechanical connections
benefits: Maintains eligibility for historic tax credits
Preserves original material whenever possible
Reversible methods allow future conservation
Meets strict historic district commission requirements

faq.question: Can you match old wrought iron finishes?
faq.answer: We use traditional linseed oil paints and hand-rubbed wax patinas to replicate original appearances without modern coatings.

faq.question: Do you work with preservation architects?
faq.answer: Yes - we regularly collaborate with firms like Quinn Evans on Detroit landmark projects.

services.name: Emergency Welding Services
services.shortDescription: 24/7 response when critical welds fail in Metro Detroit.
services.description:
When a snow-laden fire escape pulls loose or a salt-weakened beam threatens collapse, we're Detroit's go-to emergency welders.

Our rapid response trucks carry everything needed for winter field repairs: portable preheaters for subzero temps, structural epoxy for immediate load transfer, and temporary shoring systems. We've stabilized everything from broken water main supports to damaged bridge bearings during polar vortex events.

The process starts with a safety assessment - we won't put workers at risk. Then we implement temporary stabilization until permanent repairs can be scheduled. All emergency work includes follow-up inspections after temperature extremes to verify integrity.

Available 365 days/year with average 4-hour response times in Wayne, Oakland, and Washtenaw counties.
process: Step 1: Immediate hazard assessment and temporary stabilization
Step 2: Engineering consultation if structural calculations are needed
Step 3: Field welding with climate-appropriate procedures
Step 4: Follow-up inspection after extreme weather events
benefits: Prevents secondary damage from structural failures
Meets OSHA requirements for emergency repairs
Cold-weather certified welders
Coordination with local building officials

faq.question: What's your winter response capability?
faq.answer: Our trucks carry portable shelters, diesel heaters, and cold-weather welding gear for repairs down to -20°F wind chill.

faq.question: Do you handle insurance claims?
faq.answer: We provide detailed documentation and photos to support your property damage claims.

reviews.name: James R.
reviews.text: Legacy saved our Palmer Woods carriage house when inspection revealed corroded beam connections. They matched the original 1920s steel profile perfectly and their zinc coating system looks like it'll last another century. The crew even worked weekends to finish before our daughter's wedding.

reviews.name: Detroit Historical Society
reviews.text: Their work on the 1890s iron fence at Elmwood Cemetery was museum-quality. They documented every original joint before disassembly and used period-correct forge welding techniques. Five years later with Michigan winters, the repairs still look pristine.

reviews.name: Troy Medical Center
reviews.text: When we needed OR suite modifications, Legacy's certified welders followed strict infection control protocols. Their stainless steel work passed joint inspections on first attempt, avoiding costly delays. Project manager kept us updated daily.

reviews.name: Sarah K.
reviews.text: Our Rochester Hills lake house needed railings that could handle ice and kids. Legacy designed aluminum balusters with hidden drainage channels that don't ice up like our old steel ones. The powder coating matches our trim perfectly after two winters.

reviews.name: Midtown Development Co.
reviews.text: At 3AM during last January's ice storm, a failed weld on our construction crane needed immediate repair. Legacy had a crew onsite within 90 minutes with proper cold-weather gear. Their temporary fix held until we could schedule permanent repairs.

reviews.name: Ann Arbor Landlords Assoc.
reviews.text: Legacy reinforced 14 student housing balconies in one summer. Their engineered solution addressed rust jacking without requiring full replacement. Two years later with heavy use, zero callbacks - unheard of in this market.

service-areas.title: Welding Service Areas | Detroit Metro
service-areas.description: Serving Detroit, Ann Arbor, Troy, Royal Oak, and Rochester Hills with local welding expertise.

serviceAreas.content:
Legacy Welding proudly serves the Ann Arbor community, providing expert welding services for residential and commercial projects. Known for its diverse housing styles, Ann Arbor's mix of historic and modern structures requires specialized welding solutions. Whether it's repairing wrought iron railings on classic homes or fabricating custom metalwork for new developments, our team delivers precision and durability.

With cold, snowy winters and lake-effect snow, Ann Arbor's climate demands welding work that can withstand harsh conditions. We specialize in weather-resistant welding techniques to ensure your metal structures remain strong and safe year-round. From university-area properties to downtown businesses, Legacy Welding is your trusted partner for all welding needs in Ann Arbor.
serviceAreas.localContext: Ann Arbor is home to the University of Michigan, featuring a mix of historic and modern architecture with a vibrant, diverse community.

serviceAreas.content:

Troy's cold winters and lake-effect snow require durable welding solutions.
